**Reasoning:**

**OBSERVATION ACCURACY: inaccurate**
- Point 5 claims "Deduplication by timestamp" using "publication timestamp as unique ID to prevent duplicate entries" - this is **false**. The ground truth (gap_3) explicitly states there is NO deduplication and entries at the same second will collide.
- Point 6 claims "Persistent storage: Caches parsed feeds" - this is misleading. Ground truth (gap_7) states there is "No persistent state" and "Each call overwrites the entire category JSON file" (gap_3). This isn't caching; it's just output.
- Point 10 mentions handling "basic error cases (missing timestamps, network failures)" - but ground truth (gap_1, gap_8) states the bare except calls sys.exit(), killing the entire process, not "handling" errors.

**GAP COVERAGE:**
- **gap_1 (HTTP error handling)**: YES - Triage P0.1 identifies "Network failures...cause silent data loss" and P1.7 mentions "sys.exit(0) on error"
- **gap_2 (feed validation)**: YES - Triage P1.6 "No feed validation: Accepts any URL; doesn't verify feed format"
- **gap_3 (no deduplication)**: NO - The report incorrectly claims deduplication EXISTS in Observations point 5
- **gap_4 (no content extraction)**: YES - Triage P2.13 "No feed metadata preservation: Loses description, tags, enclosures, content body"
- **gap_5 (no caching/conditional fetching)**: YES - Triage P0.4 "Cache expiration with ETags/Last-Modified" and P2.11 "No incremental updates"
- **gap_6 (no rate limiting)**: YES - Triage P0.3 "No rate limiting: Simultaneous requests...will trigger 429s"
- **gap_7 (no persistent state)**: NO - The report claims persistent state exists; doesn't identify this gap
- **gap_8 (no error recovery/retry)**: YES - Triage P0.1 "No error recovery or retry logic"
- **gap_9 (no OPML/feed discovery)**: NO - Not mentioned anywhere
- **gap_10 (no output format flexibility)**: NO - Not mentioned anywhere
